title: Love At First Fight

jokes.text: I know how desperate I can get. How about you, Carlton?
jokes.setup: Will asks about their desperation levels for dates
jokes.punchline: Turning the question to Carlton to highlight his desperation
jokes.explanation: Simple setup for Carlton's response, decent character work but predictable

jokes.text: Sky's the limit. I better go lock up my Barbies.
jokes.setup: Carlton admits his desperation has no limits
jokes.punchline: Ashley fears for her dolls, suggesting Carlton's standards are extremely low
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and economy - Ashley's line is perfectly timed and absurd

jokes.text: Last Friday, the school nurse started looking good to me.
jokes.setup: Carlton discussing how long it's been since he had a girlfriend
jokes.punchline: His standards have dropped so low the school nurse looks attractive
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific desperation joke, well-delivered

jokes.text: Yeah, something about how them fat ankles roll over them squeaky white shoes.
jokes.setup: Carlton finding the school nurse attractive
jokes.punchline: Will adds grotesque specific details about her appearance
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that escalates Carlton's joke effectively

jokes.text: Red flag.
jokes.setup: Vivian describes Kayla as a 'lovely girl'
jokes.punchline: Will immediately recognizes this as coded language
jokes.explanation: Great economy and character awareness - Will knows the code

jokes.text: When she says, 'lovely girl' that means 'get out the Milk Bones.'
jokes.setup: Will explaining mother's secret code
jokes.punchline: Comparing unattractive girls to dogs needing treats
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that's both mean and funny, good structure

jokes.text: Nice personality means she's protected by Greenpeace.
jokes.setup: Vivian says Kayla has a 'nice personality'
jokes.punchline: Will translates this as meaning she's whale-sized
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy - connects 'nice personality' to environmental protection in a clever way

jokes.text: There you have it. We wouldn't have nothing to talk about. I'm not that good at math and I don't speak no French.
jokes.setup: Vivian lists Kayla's academic achievements
jokes.punchline: Will's self-deprecating assessment of his own limitations
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Will's insecurity but predictable structure

jokes.text: Voulez-vous coucher avec moi ce soir?
jokes.setup: Everyone expecting an unattractive girl based on descriptions
jokes.punchline: Kayla arrives speaking seductive French, subverting expectations
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that subverts all the setup about her appearance, great structural payoff

jokes.text: Where abouts are you from? - Harlem.
jokes.setup: Carlton romantically listing upscale NYC locations
jokes.punchline: Kayla's one-word response deflates his assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy showing Carlton's assumptions vs reality, great economy

jokes.text: Is it nice?
jokes.setup: Kayla mentions she's from Harlem
jokes.punchline: Carlton's awkward, tone-deaf follow-up question
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Carlton's sheltered perspective, great character work

jokes.text: But we have more in common. We're both attractive human beings of normal height.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to find common ground with Kayla
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous attempt to highlight their shared 'normal height'
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic that's perfectly Carlton - his desperate attempt at connection is both pathetic and funny

jokes.text: Geoffrey? He's down with my posse. He cuts a nice fade, so I keep him around. Don't that make you large.
jokes.setup: Kayla asks who the English butler is
jokes.punchline: Will pretends Geoffrey is his barber and crew member
jokes.explanation: Classic Will trying to front, good absurdist elements mixing street culture with butler reality

jokes.text: The word is 'diss.' I don't care whether it's diss, dat, or the other thing.
jokes.setup: Will corrects Carlton's pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dismissive response shows he doesn't care about getting slang right
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the malapropism, shows Carlton's stubborn character

jokes.text: Where we come from age don't make no difference. 17, 21, 33, they're all in the same grade.
jokes.setup: Will explaining street dating dynamics
jokes.punchline: Implies poor education system where everyone's held back multiple times
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about educational inequality that's both funny and pointed

jokes.text: You know, that's surprising, because sequels tend to be a little disappointing.
jokes.setup: Kayla mentions Henry V as her favorite Shakespeare play
jokes.punchline: Will treats it like a movie sequel
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection treating classical literature like Hollywood franchises

jokes.text: Which is based on the intellectual superiority of servants over those they serve.
jokes.setup: Carlton discussing Romeo and Juliet
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong interpretation of the play's themes
jokes.explanation: Shows Carlton's pretentious but wrong literary analysis, good character work

jokes.text: Mother, are you kidding? Mel Gibson in tights?
jokes.setup: Asked about her favorite part of Hamlet
jokes.punchline: Focuses on the movie star's appearance rather than the literature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary response - shallow and appearance-focused rather than intellectual

jokes.text: The Intelligent Hoodlum. I just got his tape. It's upstairs. Dude be dropping science.
jokes.setup: Discussion about poetry
jokes.punchline: Will suggests a rapper as serious poet, mixing academic and street language
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will trying to bridge his two worlds

jokes.text: In our building in West Philly, we had a wild shrew once. Just crawling out from under the kitchen sink. Yo, dude was, like, this big. Stole a Smithfield ham from my Uncle Leroy.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Shakespeare's 'Taming of the Shrew'
jokes.punchline: Will tells story about an actual rodent shrew in his building
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection from literary shrew to actual rodent, with absurd escalating details

jokes.text: I did not come to discuss Rocky, rap, and rodents.
jokes.setup: Will's increasingly off-topic contributions to academic discussion
jokes.punchline: Kayla's alliterative summary of Will's ridiculous topics
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that summarizes the absurdity, great alliteration and economy

jokes.text: Worked like a charm, Will.
jokes.setup: Will's disastrous attempt to impress Kayla
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic assessment of Will's 'success'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective sarcasm with good economy, classic Carlton smugness

jokes.text: I was always losing my car keys so I went down to the key store and had 30 copies made. That way when I lose a key, I always have another one. And next month, I do the same thing.
jokes.setup: Hilary explaining her system for not losing keys
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical solution that creates more problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - absurd solution that misses the point entirely

jokes.text: You mean, there are copies of our house key all over Los Angeles? Don't worry, Daddy. I put our name and address on all of them.
jokes.setup: Philip realizing the security implications of Hilary's key system
jokes.punchline: Hilary's 'solution' makes the security problem even worse
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes the absurd situation even more absurd, perfect Hilary moment

jokes.text: You know what my beef with Kayla is? I thought you didn't want to talk about Kayla?
jokes.setup: Will says he doesn't want to hear Kayla's name, then immediately brings her up
jokes.punchline: Carlton catches Will's contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's inability to stop thinking about her

jokes.text: Maybe we should just drive around town and look for Hilary's keys.
jokes.setup: The ongoing search for car keys
jokes.punchline: Kayla suggests the impossibly inefficient solution
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to the key situation but not particularly original

jokes.text: But you're the one fronting like you all that when in actuality, you're about that much of that.
jokes.setup: Kayla calling Will stuck-up
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate street comeback with hand gestures
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Will's street attitude coming out

jokes.text: You so stuck-up your fingers got to make an appointment to scratch your damn head.
jokes.setup: The stuck-up insult battle
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of someone too uptight for basic bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist imagery that's both original and perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Your mama's so fat, they showed her a picture of her feet and she couldn't identify them.
jokes.setup: Moving into 'your mama' jokes territory
jokes.punchline: Classic fat joke with good visual imagery
jokes.explanation: Solid 'your mama' joke structure but fairly standard content

jokes.text: Your mama's so dumb she went to the movies. It said, 'Under 17 not admitted' so she went and got 16 of her friends.
jokes.setup: Escalating the mama jokes battle
jokes.punchline: Classic dumb joke with good mathematical logic
jokes.explanation: Well-known mama joke but well-delivered and fits the moment

jokes.text: You're so ugly, she tied a pork chop around your neck just so the dog would play with you.
jokes.setup: Escalating from mama jokes to direct insults
jokes.punchline: Classic ugly joke with vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Another classic insult joke, well-known but effective in context

jokes.text: You so ugly your mama had to feed you with a slingshot.
jokes.setup: Continuing the ugly jokes battle
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of being too ugly to feed normally
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with strong visual imagery and excellent economy

jokes.text: You so ugly, that ain't a fade on your head, that's your hair running away from your face.
jokes.setup: Continuing the ugly insults
jokes.punchline: Creative explanation for Will's haircut as hair fleeing his face
jokes.explanation: More creative than typical ugly jokes, good visual imagery

jokes.text: Damn, baby. You so fine.
jokes.setup: In the middle of trading ugly insults
jokes.punchline: Will suddenly switches to attraction/compliments
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that completely subverts the insult battle into romantic attraction - excellent timing and structure

jokes.text: I would kiss you but I just had a chilidog.
jokes.setup: The romantic moment building
jokes.punchline: Will's consideration for breath after chilidog
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that maintains realism in romantic scene

jokes.text: Yeah, that's what my neighbor's gardener said.
jokes.setup: Kayla saying he shouldn't have gotten the flowers
jokes.punchline: Reveals he stole them from neighbor's garden
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of romantic gesture expectation, excellent economy

jokes.text: But I once got up to the third level of Super Mario Bros and, yo, it was hectic up there.
jokes.setup: Kayla talking about feeling intellectually overwhelmed
jokes.punchline: Will compares it to video game difficulty
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's different frame of reference for 'mental challenges'

jokes.text: When you start using them million dollar words makes my liver quiver.
jokes.setup: Will complimenting Kayla's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Street slang mixed with anatomical poetry
jokes.explanation: Good character voice mixing romance with street language

jokes.text: You're the only boy I've ever been with that really appreciates my being sesquipedalian. Meow, baby.
jokes.setup: Kayla using a big word (sesquipedalian = using big words)
jokes.punchline: Will's enthusiastic but uncomprehending response
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay joke - sesquipedalian is itself a big word for using big words, Will's response is perfect

jokes.text: Yeah, Sigma Fly.
jokes.setup: Will claiming to be in a fraternity
jokes.punchline: Made-up fraternity name 'Sigma Fly' mixing Greek letters with street slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of academic culture with street slang, great character moment

jokes.text: I thought we'd light a fire, put on a Tom Jones album and after a cognac or two test the waters of adventure. Did you know, my dear, I'm an excellent photographer? I hope you're not camera-shy.
jokes.setup: Carlton on the phone trying to sound seductive
jokes.punchline: Creepy pickup lines delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton trying to be smooth but coming off as creepy instead

jokes.text: So the draperies will be ready Thursday? Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Carlton's seductive phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was talking to interior decorator, not a woman
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that recontextualizes the entire seductive phone call

jokes.text: This is just like Cher and that bagel boy.
jokes.setup: Carlton commenting on Will dating an older woman
jokes.punchline: Reference to Cher dating much younger men
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference but fairly predictable and not particularly clever

jokes.text: That's really pitiful, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Carlton's admission of creepy behavior
jokes.punchline: His self-aware recognition of how pathetic he sounds
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware follow-up that acknowledges the patheticness

jokes.text: I'm sort of like a big old bag of Lay's potato chips. It's like, once you get a taste, you just can't stop.
jokes.setup: Vivian noting Kayla spends a lot of time with Will
jokes.punchline: Will compares himself to addictive snack food
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comparison that fits Will's confidence and humor style

jokes.text: You're certainly like a big old bag of something.
jokes.setup: Will's potato chip comparison
jokes.punchline: Vivian's cutting comeback that implies something less pleasant
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback with excellent economy and timing, cuts Will down effectively

jokes.text: I've been here all these months chasing chicken-headed girls around
jokes.setup: Will complaining about having to leave Kayla alone
jokes.punchline: Describes previous dating targets as 'chicken-headed'
jokes.explanation: Street slang that fits character but not particularly original

jokes.text: This might be the difference between Dr. Kayla Samuels who runs the hospital or just Kayla who cleans it.
jokes.setup: Vivian explaining the stakes of Kayla's education
jokes.punchline: Stark contrast between doctor and janitor futures
jokes.explanation: Good structure that effectively communicates the stakes with clear contrast

jokes.text: And I thought Carlton's come-on lines were funky.
jokes.setup: Professor using awkward Chaucer quote as pickup line
jokes.punchline: Will comparing it unfavorably to Carlton's bad lines
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Carlton's earlier creepy phone call, decent comparison

jokes.text: The answer is 'C.' I'm not sure what you're studying but I know that with multiple-choice you can't go wrong with 'C.'
jokes.setup: Will trying to help sulking Kayla study
jokes.punchline: Offers dubious test-taking strategy instead of actual help
jokes.explanation: Common test-taking joke but fits Will's character trying to help

jokes.text: Wonder how come she never cleaned my room.
jokes.setup: Learning Aunt Viv used to clean hotel rooms
jokes.punchline: Will's selfish takeaway from the revelation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's self-centered perspective even during serious moments

jokes.text: If there's anything I can do or not do, I will do or not do.
jokes.setup: Will offering to help Kayla with her studies
jokes.punchline: Gets tongue-tied trying to be supportive
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's nervousness affecting his usual smooth talking

jokes.text: Out!
jokes.setup: Will and Kayla's final romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Will presumably gets thrown out or leaves abruptly
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy ending but effective for closing the scene

tmdb_overview: Jasmine Guy plays a scholarship student whose introduction to Will opens up a different world of fun and games - and romance - to divert her from her studies.
